feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: mythos — MITH oss
entry.summary: mythos is pronounced "MITH oss" (/ˈmɪθɑs/). GenAm "MITH-ahs" (Merriam-Webster primary). Greek-derived term for a culture's underlying narrative/belief system. One reading pinned per request.

entry.title: I-JEPA — eye jep uh
entry.summary: I-JEPA is pronounced "eye jep uh" (/ˌaɪ ˈdʒɛpə/). Meta's image self-supervised model (Image JEPA); the 'I' is the letter 'eye' /aɪ/ and JEPA is one word /ˈdʒɛpə/ rhyming with 'pepper', so 'eye-JEP-uh' — the way LeCun voices the suffix. Predicts in latent space, not pixels.

entry.title: V-JEPA — vee jep uh
entry.summary: V-JEPA is pronounced "vee jep uh" (/ˌviː ˈdʒɛpə/). Meta's video JEPA; 'V' (Video) is spelled 'vee' and JEPA is said as the word, giving 'vee-JEP-uh'. Not letter-by-letter.

entry.title: V-JEPA 2 — vee jep uh two
entry.summary: V-JEPA 2 is pronounced "vee jep uh two" (/ˌviː ˈdʒɛpə ˈtuː/). 2025 video world model, successor to V-JEPA; numeral read 'two' — 'vee-JEP-uh two'.

entry.title: A-JEPA — ay jep uh
entry.summary: A-JEPA is pronounced "ay jep uh" (/ˌeɪ ˈdʒɛpə/). Audio/spectrogram JEPA built on I-JEPA; the 'A' (Audio) is the letter-name 'ay' /eɪ/, NOT the article 'uh' — 'ay-JEP-uh'.

entry.title: MC-JEPA — em see jep uh
entry.summary: MC-JEPA is pronounced "em see jep uh" (/ˌɛm ˌsiː ˈdʒɛpə/). Motion-and-Content JEPA; the initials have no vowel so they are letter-spelled 'em-see' ahead of the word JEPA — 'em-see-JEP-uh'.

entry.title: Point-JEPA — point jep uh
entry.summary: Point-JEPA is pronounced "point jep uh" (/ˌpɔɪnt ˈdʒɛpə/). JEPA for point clouds; ordinary word 'point' + JEPA — 'point-JEP-uh'.

entry.title: DINOv2 — dee noh vee two
entry.summary: DINOv2 is pronounced "dee noh vee two" (/ˈdiːnoʊ viː ˈtuː/). Meta/FAIR self-supervised ViT foundation model, DINO scaled to v2; said 'DEE-noh vee-two' (DINO = self-DIstillation with NO labels). A minority says 'DYE-noh'.

entry.title: DINOv3 — dee noh vee three
entry.summary: DINOv3 is pronounced "dee noh vee three" (/ˈdiːnoʊ viː ˈθriː/). Meta's 2025 ~7B-param self-supervised vision backbone, third in the DINO line; 'DEE-noh vee-three' is most common, but the dinosaur-pun reading 'DYE-noh' is genuinely widespread, so the vowel is unsettled.

entry.title: iBOT — eye bot
entry.summary: iBOT is pronounced "eye bot" (/ˈaɪbɑːt/). ByteDance image-BERT pretraining with an online tokenizer (i = image, lowercase like iPhone); the repo's robot mascot signals 'EYE-bot'.

entry.title: BEiT — beet
entry.summary: BEiT is pronounced "beet" (/biːt/). Microsoft masked-image-modeling pretraining (BERT for image Transformers, i = image); no official reading — split between 'beet' (most common, one syllable) and 'bait'.

entry.title: DINO-WM — dee noh W M
entry.summary: DINO-WM is pronounced "dee noh W M" (/ˈdiːnoʊ ˌdʌbəljuː ˈɛm/). NYU/Meta-FAIR latent world model built on frozen DINOv2 patch features for zero-shot planning; said 'DEE-noh W-M', often expanded to 'dino world model'.

entry.title: BYOL — B Y O L
entry.summary: BYOL is pronounced "B Y O L" (/ˌbiː waɪ oʊ ˈɛl/). Negative-pair-free SSL that bootstraps a target network from an online network (DeepMind 2020); the authors fixed no pronunciation, so it is both spelled out 'B-Y-O-L' and said as a word 'bee-OHL' — the follow-up BYOL-A audio paper even puns 'pronounced viola'.

entry.title: SimSiam — sim sigh am
entry.summary: SimSiam is pronounced "sim sigh am" (/sɪmˈsaɪæm/). Negative-free Siamese SSL that avoids collapse via a stop-gradient; portmanteau of 'Simple Siamese', so the tail is read like 'Siam' — 'sim-SY-am'.

entry.title: VICReg — vik reg
entry.summary: VICReg is pronounced "vik reg" (/ˈvɪkrɛɡ/). SSL that prevents collapse with separate Variance-Invariance-Covariance Regularization terms; said as the word 'VIK-reg' (hard g from 'regularization'), not spelled out.

entry.title: VICRegL — vik reg el
entry.summary: VICRegL is pronounced "vik reg el" (/ˈvɪkrɛɡ ˌɛl/). VICReg extended to learn local/dense visual features for detection and segmentation; 'VIK-reg' followed by a spelled-out 'L' (for Local).

entry.title: Barlow Twins — bar loh twinz
entry.summary: Barlow Twins is pronounced "bar loh twinz" (/ˈbɑːrloʊ ˈtwɪnz/). SSL whose loss drives the cross-correlation matrix of two identical network 'twins' toward the identity; plain English, named for neuroscientist Horace Barlow's redundancy-reduction principle — 'BAR-loh twinz'.

entry.title: MoCo — moh koh
entry.summary: MoCo is pronounced "moh koh" (/ˈmoʊkoʊ/). FAIR contrastive SSL with a momentum-updated encoder and a queue of negatives; a blend of MOmentum + COntrast, said as a word — 'MOH-koh'.

entry.title: SimCLR — sim clear
entry.summary: SimCLR is pronounced "sim clear" (/sɪmˈklɪr/). Google/Hinton contrastive SSL framework; 'Sim' (Simple) + 'CLR' (Contrastive Learning of Representations) puns on 'clear', so 'sim-CLEAR' dominates; a minority spells it 'sim-C-L-R'.

entry.title: MAE — may
entry.summary: MAE is pronounced "may" (/meɪ/). FAIR masked-image SSL (Masked AutoEncoder) that reconstructs pixels — the model JEPA argues against. Genuinely unsettled: many say the word 'may', others spell 'em-ay-EE'; no author statement, and it collides with 'mean absolute error'.

entry.title: data2vec — day tuh too vek
entry.summary: data2vec is pronounced "day tuh too vek" (/ˈdeɪtə tuː vɛk/). Meta AI cross-modal SSL that predicts its own latent representations (closest non-JEPA cousin in spirit); inherits the word2vec/wav2vec lineage, so '2' is 'to' and 'vec' is 'vek' — 'DAY-tuh-too-vek'.

entry.title: IRIS — eye ris
entry.summary: IRIS is pronounced "eye ris" (/ˈaɪrɪs/). Sample-efficient Atari agent that learns inside a discrete-autoencoder + GPT-like Transformer world model; styled as the real word 'iris' (the official repo is lowercase) — 'EYE-ris', not lettered out.

entry.title: TWM — T W M
entry.summary: TWM is pronounced "T W M" (/ˌtiː ˌdʌbəljuː ˈɛm/). A Transformer-XL-based world model for data-efficient Atari RL; the initials have no vowel to form a word, so they are spelled out letter by letter — 'T-W-M'.

entry.title: Genie 2 — jee nee two
entry.summary: Genie 2 is pronounced "jee nee two" (/ˈdʒiːni ˈtuː/). DeepMind's large-scale foundation world model that generates action-controllable, playable 3D environments from a single image; 'Genie' (from 'Generative Interactive Environments') is said like the common word genie, '2' as 'two' — 'JEE-nee two'.
